parsing_approach: Not a parser: an independent verifier. Derives every gate score from named, measured checks — recounts dangling edges, re-resolves every provenance reference into the source graph and onto disk, recomputes inventory hashes, re-runs each registered parser's smoke test, double-runs parsers on repository samples for determinism, cross-checks every node's parser_id against the registry and its input patterns, compares gallery copies byte-for-byte when no adaptations are claimed, re-resolves every documentation claim's excerpt onto its cited span, enforces the authority rule (a node validated/accepted on asserted evidence alone fails the gate), re-resolves observed evidence (recomputes trace/screenshot hashes, resolves journey_refs, matches walked routes against parsed Route nodes, recomputes the committed DB hash to prove the source tree untouched), and enforces the RT-8 reproducibility split (volatile content in runtime artefacts fails; the normalization rule is written in journeys.schema.json). Scores are fixed formulas over check outcomes; there are no assertable score constants.
extracts: verification.json with all ten gate dimensions as {value, derived_from, measurement} objects
checks[] with each check tied to the score it feeds
self_test result proving the gate can fail (mutation testing over artefact copies)
known_limitations: Structural verification: it proves artefacts are consistent, grounded, and reproducible — it cannot judge whether a semantic interpretation is the *best* one; that remains reviewer work.
Report section check is heading-based; it verifies presence and status consistency, not prose quality.
Evidence span checks verify files exist and spans fit within them, not that the span's text semantically supports the claim.
Runs read-only over the repository and writes only .work/semantic-loop/verification.json.
